title: Request to Support Z-Image Controlnet
body: ### Feature Idea Alibaba-PAI just released a new Controlnet model for Z-Image-Turbo (https://huggingface.co/alibaba-pai/Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union). But I think ComfyUI doesn’t support it yet. When I tried to load it, I got this error: ``` got prompt error could not detect control model type. error checkpoint does not contain controlnet or t2i adapter data F:\AI\Stability Matrix\Models\ControlNet\Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union.safetensors !!! Exception during processing !!! ERROR: controlnet file is invalid and does not contain a valid controlnet model. Traceback (most recent call last): File "F:\AI\ComfyUI-Nightly\ComfyUI\execution.py", line 510, in execute output_data, output_ui, has_subgraph, has_pending_tasks = await get_output_data(prompt_id, unique_id, obj, input_data_all, execution_block_cb=execution_block_cb, pre_execute_cb=pre_execute_cb, hidden_inputs=hidden_inputs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "F:\AI\ComfyUI-Nightly\ComfyUI\execution.py", line 324, in get_output_data return_values = await _async_map_node_over_list(prompt_id, unique_id, obj, input_data_all, obj.FUNCTION, allow_interrupt=True, execution_block_cb=execution_block_cb, pre_execute_cb=pre_execute_cb, hidden_inputs=hidden_inputs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "F:\AI\ComfyUI-Nightly\ComfyUI\execution.py", line 298, in _async_map_node_over_list await process_inputs(input_dict, i) File "F:\AI\ComfyUI-Nightly\ComfyUI\execution.py", line 286, in process_inputs result = f(**inputs) ^^^^^^^^^^^ File "F:\AI\ComfyUI-Nightly\ComfyUI\nodes.py", line 815, in load_controlnet raise RuntimeError("ERROR: controlnet file is invalid and does not contain a valid controlnet model.") RuntimeError: ERROR: controlnet file is invalid and does not contain a valid controlnet model. ``` ### Existing Solutions _No response_ ### Other _No response_

title: Feedback on update frequency and stability
body:
### Custom Node Testing

- [ ] I have tried disabling custom nodes and the issue persists (see [how to disable custom nodes](https://docs.comfy.org/troubleshooting/custom-node-issues#step-1%3A-test-with-all-custom-nodes-disabled) if you need help)

### Your question

First of all, thanks for all the work being put into ComfyUI. 
However, the current update frequency is becoming difficult to manage. There are often multiple updates in a single day (sometimes three or more through the desktop app), and with the latest updates several things broke — including nodes and basic features like copy/paste, user session on restart, nodes map nothing working

Frequent updates are great for development, but for users who rely on ComfyUI in active workflows, stability is far more important than constant changes.

It might help the community if the project focused more on stability and compatibility rather than pushing multiple updates per day. For example:

Fewer releases but with more testing

A more stable release channel

Better compatibility checks with popular nodes before updates

Right now many users end up with broken nodes or workflows after updating, which disrupts productivity.

Again, I really appreciate the work being done on ComfyUI — this feedback is meant to help make the experience more stable for everyday users.

### Logs

```powershell

```

### Other

_No response_

title: feat: LoRA as prompt
body: Hi! As we know, in A1111 webui, LoRA(and LyCORIS) is used as prompt. IMHO, LoRA as a prompt (as well as node) can be convenient. ## Simplicity When using many LoRAs(e.g. for character, fashion, background, etc), it becomes easily bloated. Text prompts can reduce this. ## Sharability People tend to share EXIF in A1111 format. Text prompt is good for copy-and-paste. Sometimes what users want is getting started as rapid as possible. That's because they are so excited by new ideas that just checking out the result ASAP has higher priority. ## Experiment Users of ComfyUI are more hard-core than those of A1111. They experiment a lot. In many cases, text is faster to edit (with autocompletion or text editors). For example, on A1111 webui, I use find-and-replace feature in VSCode for automatically replacing multiple LoRA weights at once. What's more, I "generate" a text of list of LoRA with specific weights and additional args from a simple script(like .js, .py, or .sh) for experiments. This practice saves time a lot and thus essential in my workflow. ## Conclusion I like node-based workflow. But things especially like LoRA can benefit us by being prompt. Prompt grammar is DSL, and well-defined DSL becomes really productive. How do you think? Thank you :)

title: [Announcement] Execution Model Inversion PR Will Be Merged Soon
body:
PR 2666, "Execution Model Inversion", <https://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI/pull/2666> (*aka the PR that adds the core engine necessary for things like in-workflow for loops*), will be merged **Next Week**! This is final public notice to please test this PR ASAP!

If you are a nodepack author, especially if your pack monkeypatches the core or touches complex execution logic, please test compatibility asap!
We're hoping for a smooth merge that doesn't break anything for anyone here!

### What does this mean for regular users?

Initially, if all goes well: Nothing! Everything should continue to work exactly the same as it does.

In time: this opens up a much more powerful core execution engine that nodepack authors can take advantage of, and then in turn workflow authors can use to make much more complex workflows.
Guill has a demo pack here: <https://github.com/BadCafeCode/execution-inversion-demo-comfyui>

I've attached to this announcement a couple gifs showing demo nodes implementing a primitive for loop and a primitive list accumulator, examples of the types of new tools that will be made available in ComfyUI thanks to this PR.

*This post has also been sent to Reddit <https://new.reddit.com/r/comfyui/comments/1ekz6sd/comfyui_announcement_pr_2666_execution_model/>*

(If you're excited about the new possibilities here, be sure to thank @guill for this amazing PR!)
![loopy](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/953c76e2-91b2-4dad-8348-f981fd948e39)
![accum](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/abc88f61-8474-4c67-930d-0b62ad419645)

title: + frame selection
body:
standard for node graph is : left dragging in the canvas = frame selection
but LMB is used to scroll canvas
so suggested change is: MMB to scroll canvas (every mouse has a clickable scroll wheel these days)
and LMB on canvas = drag a frame to select nodes.

title: Consistency.
body:
It would be nice to have more consistency throughout the interface and follow node graph standards.

1. ctrl+d is usually duplicate node, never "load default graph"
2. link extremity usually snaps when the mouse cursor is a few pixels around #in
3. when pulling a link, all incompatible #in dim out
4. panning is usually middle mouse drag, because then it doesn't conflict with node move or link pull, also it's more consistent with scrollwheel zoom
5. when clicking on a number field, the common behavior is that field is selected, this allows overwrite of value without drag slide

title: Add native support for LLMs
body:
### Feature Idea

Hello, can you add native nodes for support of LLMs, like gemma, qwen, maybe others. They can be used as prompt enhancers, smart modifiers of prompt or other parameters image captioners etc. There are already the same models used as encoders , so maybe it will be not so hard to just add a LLM inference node

### Existing Solutions

There are extensions, that mostly use open ai compatible endpoints, that make them generally useless if the llm server on the same machine

There are few extensions with inference inside them, it's still would be nice to see native comfy ui support:
- https://github.com/alexcong/ComfyUI_QwenVL
- https://github.com/1038lab/ComfyUI-QwenVL
- https://github.com/SXQBW/ComfyUI-Qwen-Omni

title: Phantom (base on wan2.1) support
body:
### Feature Idea

> Phantom is a unified video generation framework for single and multi-subject references, built on existing text-to-video and image-to-video architectures. It achieves cross-modal alignment using text-image-video triplet data by redesigning the joint text-image injection model. Additionally, it emphasizes subject consistency in human generation while enhancing ID-preserving video generation.

https://github.com/Phantom-video/Phantom

Although it was supported in wanwrapper of kijai, I think it would be great to have comfyui native support that we can use GGUF version to reduce VRAM consumption and other features.

### Existing Solutions

_No response_

### Other

_No response_

title: [Request] cudaMallocAsync support for checkPoolLiveAllocations for use in FBCache + Compile model max-autotune mode
body:
### Your question

When using FBCache + Compiler, if compile model is set to max-autotune, KSampler will crash with the following error: cudaMallocAsync does not yet support checkPoolLiveAllocations

Steps to replicate
Launch comfyUI (by default it seems to launch with cudaMallocAsync)
Install wavespeed and/ or teacache
Add their FBCache node and *compiler*
Set compiler to autotune (if using wavespeed compiler, in the [mode] box type max-autotune)
                                          If using teacache node, set mode to max-autotune
Link everything to KSampler
Load SDXL Model
Generate image

Get error

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28773844-a39f-4b16-ba27-fdf314fe223c)

Pytorch 2.6.0+cu12.6

title: Support Kandinsky 5 - *Image*
body:
### Feature Idea

Despite Kandinsky 5.0 being known for the recent text/image2video model, they actually released an image generation model a day ago. This model is a 6B DiT capable of generating images and its derivative is capable of image *editing*.

Flux VAE is used for image encoding/decoding and Qwen2.5-VL with CLIP for comprehensive image understanding. Both components are already present in Comfy's codebase. The model is multilingual.

The model checkpoints:

https://huggingface.co/kandinskylab/Kandinsky-5.0-T2I-Lite-sft-Diffusers

https://huggingface.co/kandinskylab/Kandinsky-5.0-I2I-Lite-sft-Diffusers

The technical report:

https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.14993

### Existing Solutions

_No response_

### Other

_No response_

title: ComfyUI keeps loading no or outdated workflows on startup
body:
### Custom Node Testing

- [x] I have tried disabling custom nodes and the issue persists (see [how to disable custom nodes](https://docs.comfy.org/troubleshooting/custom-node-issues#step-1%3A-test-with-all-custom-nodes-disabled) if you need help)

### Your question

With the option of session restore active, ComfyUI should open with the last saved state of the last used workflow (used to work consistently until several months ago).

But since an update late last year, ComfyUI opens with either the basic default workflow, or some old version of the last used workflow. Said old version can be from hours before the last time I actually saved it, or in one case even an entire day older. When I carefully close that version of the workflow without saving and load it again, it opens the correct version, so it seems to load some messed up cached version instead from who knows where.

In one potentially disastrous case (not this time due to being able to extract the last version from a generated image, but still), after the latest update it outright overwrote my saved workflow with an outdated version.

Did ComfyUI at some point change the way it caches sessions, or saves cached old versions of workflows somewhere, and refers to those instead of the actual version saved in my user folder? It's extremely annoying behavior, and makes the whole "restore session" feature essentially useless.

### Logs

```powershell

```

### Other

_No response_
